item.title: Chuck Berry
item.description: Genres: Rock & roll, rhythm & blues Scene: Chicago (Chess Records) Era: 1955–1964 (primary period) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The intro to “Johnny B.

item.title: Marvin Gaye
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, psychedelic soul, quiet storm Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1961–1984 (primary period: 1964–1973) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Listen to the shift between “I Heard It Through the Grapevine” (1968) and What’s Going On (1971): from a single built on paranoia and rhythmic ...

item.title: Burt Bacharach
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, traditional pop, film score Scene: Brill Building Era: 1957–2023 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Burt Bacharach is the most harmonically sophisticated songwriter in the history of American pop — the figure who brought Ravel, Debussy, and Brazilian bossa nova into the three-minute singl...

item.title: The Four Tops
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1954–present (classic era 1964–1967) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Levi Stubbs had a voice built for crisis — a baritone-tenor that could move from conversational warmth to desperate, shouting intensity with an abruptness that made every song...

item.title: The Rolling Stones
item.description: Genres: British blues, blues rock, rock & roll, roots rock Scene: London (Richmond, then Soho) Era: 1962–present (primary period: 1968–1972) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Keith Richards plays behind the beat.

item.title: The Who
item.description: Genres: Mod, freakbeat, art rock, hard rock, rock opera Scene: Mod, Shepherd’s Bush, London Era: 1964–present (primary period: 1965–1973) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Most drummers keep time.

item.title: Hill country blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Hill country blues Period: c.

item.title: Swamp blues
item.description: Blues → Electric blues → Swamp blues Period: 1954–1970 Location: Baton Rouge, recorded in Crowley, Louisiana Key influences: Electric blues, zydeco, Cajun music RYM The laziest beat in the blues: a shuffle that sounds half-asleep, drums brushed down to a whisper, tremolo guitar hanging in the humidi...

item.title: Berry Gordy
item.description: Born: November 28, 1929 (Detroit, Michigan) Active: 1957–present (label-building era: 1959–1988) Role: Label founder, songwriter, producer Key institutions: Motown Records (1959–1988), Jobete Music (publishing), Motown Productions (film/television) Wikipedia · RYM The $800 that founded Tamla Records...

item.title: Sun Records
item.description: Founded: February 1, 1952 (predecessor Memphis Recording Service, January 3, 1950) Location: Memphis, Tennessee (706 Union Avenue) Founder: Sam Phillips Key years: 1952–1968 (sold to Shelby Singleton, July 1969) Subsidiaries: Flip Records (1955), Phillips International Records (October 1957) RYM The...
